text.title: growth-affiliate-marketing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-affiliate-marketing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Two-tier (MLM-style) commission structures where affiliates earn on recruits' sales.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Attracts low-quality recruiters; creates FTC compliance risk and tax-form complexity with no proportional lift in quality referrals.

failure-mode.description: Launching the program without an asset library and expecting affiliates to create their own promotional materials.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Affiliates abandon programs that require significant creative work; they move to programs that make promotion frictionless.

failure-mode.description: Inactive affiliate tail: signing up many affiliates without a pruning process.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: 80% of affiliates produce zero sales. An unpruned roster inflates support overhead and dilutes program resources. Healthy programs continually recruit quality partners and prune inactive ones quarterly.

failure-mode.description: Paying commissions before the refund window closes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cancellations and chargebacks that arrive after payout are unrecoverable losses. The 30-day holdback is non-negotiable arithmetic, not a courtesy.
